message:
test(fuzz): add session-handshake metamorphic-relations target (resolves rnkk6)

Existing fuzz_session and fuzz_session_transcript exercise decoders
and discard accepted structures. The handshake's *invariant* relations
— what responder-picks negotiation actually means, what binds a
session MAC to a particular tuple — were not asserted across an
arbitrary input space.

Adds fuzz_session_metamorphic with 11 metamorphic relations:

  Suite negotiation (negotiate_suite):
    MR-NEG-1  idempotence
    MR-NEG-2  initiator-shuffle invariance (responder-picks invariant)
    MR-NEG-3  containment + MINIMUM_SUITE floor on Some
    MR-NEG-4  responder-priority: every earlier responder suite must
              fail the predicate (in-initiator AND >= floor)
    MR-NEG-5  empty-intersection ⇒ None

  Session MAC (compute_session_mac / verify_session_mac):
    MR-MAC-1  round-trip with matching parameters succeeds
    MR-MAC-2  mac_key bit-flip rejects (key isolation)
    MR-MAC-3  session_id bit-flip rejects (cross-session replay defense)
    MR-MAC-4  direction flip rejects (reflection-attack defense)
    MR-MAC-5  seq+1 rejects (replay defense)
    MR-MAC-6  frame_bytes bit-flip rejects (payload binding)

These are the public-contract guarantees the responder-picks rule and
the session-MAC binding scheme rely on. The session-handshake worked
example in AGENTS.md cites the responder-picks invariant explicitly;
this target makes it executable across the fuzzer's full input space.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
